decisions.chosen: "detail": "Ran the ADR Review Protocol (adr-review skill) as a 6-agent debate (architect agent, critic, independent-thinker, security, analyst, high-level-advisor) on draft ADR-062. First pass blocked on a workflow args bug (draft path unreadable) but surfaced two real precedent conflicts: the #1993 Serena re-assertion hook (invoke_serena_reassertion.py:16-29) rejected stateful gating, and ADR-061 was withdrawn 2026-05-27 for premature abstraction. Revised the ADR and re-ran the verification: 4 Accept, 1 Disagree-and-Commit, 1 analyst Block on a single capability-binding sentence (closed in-favor-of-user by splitting Read-gate capability=get_symbols_overview from symbol-grep capability=definition/references). Verdict ACCEPTED-WITH-DC; timing dissent recorded per User Sovereignty (user chose hard-block enabled now)."

events.content: "Evidence": "Pre-PR checklist walked: pre_pr.py 12 PASS (Em/en-dash Prohibition, Install Parity, Plugin Version Bump, Canonical Citation all PASS); 1314 hooks tests pass; markdownlint 0 errors on changed md; no secrets; conventional commits; atomic chunks. The 2 pre_pr FAILs (Agent Drift in security/task-decomposer, markdownlint debt in build/audit/GENERATION-AUDIT.md and powershell-security-checklist.md) are PREEXISTING and unrelated to this diff."
"Evidence": "npx markdownlint-cli2 --fix on changed md (ADR-062, debate log, lsp-first.md + mirrors): 0 errors."

"detail": "Cloned nesaminua/claude-code-lsp-enforcement-kit (MIT, v2.3.2) to TMPDIR; ran a 5-reader research workflow over the kit internals and this repo's hook/rule/generation conventions plus an adversarial critique. Verdict: BUILD a Python port of a conditional, availability-gated subset (kit is JS, global, unconditional; conflicts with ADR-042, fail-open, per-repo plugin)."

"detail": "User directive: full 7-hook port, all Serena-supported languages, 3-tier fallback (Serena -> native LSP -> grep/glob/sed last), graduated hard-block enabled on merge, with kill switch + fail-open safety net."
